reviews.review_text:
We had an absolutely faultless stay!
Firstly, every staff member we encountered were so friendly and we were constantly greeted with a smile and nothing was ever too much trouble.
Our cabin was spotless on arrival and in a fantastic spot right on the billabong, great size for 2 adults and 2 kids. Beds were super comfy.
I found the activities such good value, $10 an hour for kyacks, go carts etc. We used these facilities so much.
Our children (2 and 4) loved both pools and waterpark. It’s great that one pool is heated.
Felt very safe and secure with the gated entry.
The little coffee shop on site was great and the staff there were also super friendly.
All in all, we can’t fault it and we can’t wait to come back on our next trip!

Great spot for the van. Water park, pool and play area for kids was impressive. Camp kitchen was fantastic over the water.
We booked a billabong site and it felt like we were the only ones in the park.
We will be back again.
Wonderful park! My daughter just did the kids club, she is two and it was fantastic! They made pizzas, and painted mugs. The staff was incredible! Could not fault a thing! Thank you

We stayed here for a week and what a week it was! From the lovely accomodating ladies on reception to the fantastic surroundings. Cabin  had everything you needed and the facilities are that good I’m bringing my kids there partners and grandkids back in March all the way from NZ

10/10 recommend for families with young kids. My 2 & 4yo had an absolute blast on the water park, waterslides, jumping pillow, park area. The staff were incredibly warm, welcoming and helpful! We originally booked a Kirra cabin but got upgraded to a Fingal cabin!! It was modern, spacious, clean and comfortable!
Cannot wait to spend some more time here on our next family holidays.
We had a caravan power site for 2 nights. Love the place. Great location, friendly service and high quality facility set-up. Enjoyed the stay. We will definitely come  back.🤗

Fantastic trip, great service even before we arrived, offering assistance if necessary even a free upgrade.
Wonderful ammenities and facilities available + lots of extras.
Didn’t seem like it would ever be too busy, we will definitely be back!!! Thank you

The cabin was really quite poor, their was a laminted headboard bolted to the wall, sheets had rips, blankets smelled, cockroaches on the floor, the couch and coffee  table was in bad condition, and sink plug wasn't the right one for the sink so totally useless, the TV was basic and no streaming (wet weather),  generally the cabin was old and noticeably well worn...certainly not worth $330 for the night. The park itself is well maintained, with lots of activities and the staff were very friendly,  professional and welcoming.
Noted: There were other cabins that looked in alot better condition.

Had a road trip from Melbourne to qld and we stayed at this beautiful caravan park. It was so beautified and such a resort type of vibe.
Beautiful amenities! Beautiful scenery only downfall was the mosquitoes so be prepared for that but its worth the stay

Our family stayed here for 3 nights last week over 2 ensuite sites & had an amazing time. There is so much to do, the park is beautiful & all staff we engaged with were so friendly & helpful. We will definitely be back. Thank you for a wonderful holiday. From The Rogerson’s.
Amazing stay with a large family. Children had a blast with the water slides and the pool

Stayed here over QLD labor day long weekend. The place was packed with not 1 spare spot so it's very popular and for good reason. Great location just off the highway, well maintained facilities and sites and an amazing chill vibe. The water slides and the billabong were fantastic for the kids. There was a lot of noise which was to be expected with so many kids. We have 2 young boys so it didn't worry us but if you don't like kid noise then maybe don't come on a long weekend. Overall 5 stars would be back.
We have been twice in the span of a few weeks and loved it. Something for all the kids and adults to do without leaving the park.

We try to come here every year. The facilities are always so clean, the kids activities are fantastic... especially during school holidays when they have an extra holiday program. We have stayed in the Burleigh cabin the last 2 times, and for a family of 5, the value is fantastic, it's the perfect set-up. When we stay here, we don't have to leave to look for things to do, the kids always have the best time! The addition of the Pickleball court was a massive hit with the kids! Our favourite Big4 by far!

Everything about this place screams kids paradise , which equals happy parents. They have friendly staff , verrry comfortable beds and clean cabins , and plenty for the kids to do. They had a small treat tuckshop at reception, pickle ball courts, kart hire , jumping pillow , amazing pool facilities and a lake for the kids to fish and  kayaks to hire by the hour.
We had 6 families checked in, we all had the best time and will b booking again. One of the best resorts I’ve been to .
Fantastic spot here. Can't rate it more highly. The facilities were great. Very clean as well. The staff were very helpful too. We will be coming back for sure.
The whole family had heaps of fun!! We loved the water park and the food trucks. The communal kitchen area is amazing and right next to the playground. Kids had a great time.

We travelled up from Tasmania. It was well worth the travel. We absolutely loved this park. So much to do for the kids and us, great staff and location. Highly recommend people to stay here for a great family friendly big 4. 😃

Great family friendly holiday park. It has everything you need from pools, multiple waterslides, pedal karts, kayaking, food trucks, kids club. The staff are super friendly and helpful!! Cabins were modern and clean. Would highly recommend.
This place is a hidden gem
Always a great experience kids love it only downside both times been there had a terrible bed.

What an incredible spot for families! This caravan park is right up there with some of the best—on par with Sandstone Point and Hastings (though a bar for the adults would make it perfect 😜).

The service at the front desk is truly outstanding, the best we’ve experienced, and we’ve travelled around Australia twice. The team are so welcoming, always smiling, and genuinely happy to help with anything you need. Their friendliness really makes the whole stay even more enjoyable.

Highly recommend for a fun, relaxing family getaway!

We had a fantastic stay at Big 4 Tweed Billabong Holiday Park . Our family reunion included 28 people, both adults and kids, and we stayed here over Easter from 2–7 April 2026. The facilities are excellent—the playground, water park, and especially the billabong were perfect for keeping everyone entertained. The camp kitchen was clean, well-equipped, and made meal prep easy for such a large group.
Equipment hire is very affordable, with stand-up paddleboards and pedal karts available for just $10 per hour, which the kids loved. The pickleball court was a real hit too—it’s free to use with a simple booking, which worked perfectly for our large group.
A few downsides: the plane noise can be noticeable at times, there were midges around, and the park isn’t near the beach.
Overall, we would definitely recommend this place for families and large groups. There’s plenty to do for all ages, and the facilities make it a fun and comfortable spot for a memorable holiday.

We had an absolutely fantastic stay at Tweed Billabong BIG4 and can’t recommend it highly enough.

From the moment we arrived, every single staff member was incredible—reception through to the grounds maintenance team. My son is very friendly and made it his mission to chat with everyone, and the team were always so warm and engaging with him. It really made the experience feel personal and welcoming.

The facilities were outstanding and immaculately maintained. The pool was spotless, and the entire pool area was so clean with plenty of seating available, which made it easy to relax while keeping an eye on the kids. The water park is incredible, I was blown away that a water park of that size was on site at a caravan park! The jumping pillow was a huge hit, and there were daily activities for the little ones that kept them entertained throughout our stay. The Sprinkles Bakehouse activities were a standout, such a fun and thoughtful addition.

Our cabin was excellent! clean, comfortable, and had everything we needed. The whole park is beautifully kept and incredibly peaceful, making it the perfect place to unwind.

What we loved most is that you really don’t need to leave the park if you don’t want to, it has everything for a relaxing family getaway. But if you do feel like heading out, you’re only about 12 minutes drive from Coolangatta Beach, which is super convenient.

The pops up food trucks and ice cream truck were great, especially if you didn’t feel like cooking.

Overall, the cleanliness, facilities, friendly staff, and overall experience made this exceptional value for money. Plenty for the kids to do, and just as enjoyable for the adults.

We’ll definitely be back!

This place delivers! Great price , great cabins( actually a new house not a cabin), great activities for kids. Perfect really. They could maybe deliver better on food but really that's not critical. Watch out for middies in the evening is my only concern. Excellent!

We stayed on the grass caravan sites, which were generously sized and very comfortable. Despite being right next to the highway, we were pleasantly surprised by how little road noise there was.
The water park was fantastic — both the kids and adults absolutely loved it! They also had food trucks on site on Fridays and Saturdays, which added to the great atmosphere.
Overall, it was the perfect spot for a fun birthday weekend getaway.

Positive review- great customer service, lovely workers, amazing pools and slides!! Love how shady the sites and pool areas are.
Camp kitchen 🤯 mind blowing, before views and everything you need! Tho would be nice to have just the basics in the kitchen such as dish cloths, dish liquid.

Negatives -
No bath plugs in parent's bathrooms which made the parents bathrooms useless.
Things that they can't change unfortunately are the Planes were very loud, made our 1 year old naps and sleep time very hard as she didn't like the loud noises.

Overall was a lovely stay and would definitely come back!

We absolutely loved our stay at the big4 billabong at tweed heads ,we had our caravan right on the billabong
It was central to the camp kitchen and the amenitie blocks
The pool was a very relaxing spot and cool off
On the friday and saturday nights the food trucks came which was very convenient to change of having camp food
Also the lovely ladys at the brunch van where amazing great breakfast food and of course coffee

Have stayed before and will be staying again, phenomenal holiday with all the kids. The billabong was a favorite, watched the stingrays and barracudas, live music at camp kitchen, waterslides were a hit, and customer service exceptional this tome around. A lovely New Zealand woman went above and beyond and made it tgat little bit more special. Highly recommend!
We stayed on a billabong site, fantastic. The grandkids had a ball.

Wow!!! We just finished our first stay at Tweed Big4 Billabong and holy moly, that was amazing!! We booked for my son's 6th birthday as we trade presents for experiences, and booked with some friends, and wow, what an amazing weekend. From the moment we checked in, all of the staff were just incredible. So friendly and helpful and very happy to be here helping guests. We stayed in one of the Cabarita cabins for 3 nights and it was fantastic. A new cabin that was incredibly well kept and very clean. Close to the waterpark, but easy access to everywhere. From sliding down the water slides, to riding bikes, cannoeing, playing in the playgrounds, pedal carts, mini golf, basketball and soccer, fishing... the activities were endless. The kids had an absolute ball and were completely wrecked by the end of each night. They're both asleep right now already in the car and we haven't left the carpark yet 🤣 The camp kitchen is an awesome facility, and such a beautiful communal space when staying with friends/family to meet up at meal times. The cafe was a huge bonus too, especially for the morning coffee fix. Absolutely thrilled with this experience by both kids and adults!! We already can't wait for our next visit ❤️ Thank you so much to the beautiful team, for taking such wonderful care of the grounds and facilities. What a wonderfully relaxing weekend!
Beautiful and clean facilities. Best camp kitchen I’ve ever seen. Would definitely stay again.

We stayed here in March 2026, first time we have stayed here on a powered site. The staff were incredible, so friendly and always very generous with the kids activity bookings. Our site was clean and tidy and we had the best location next to the camp kitchen which was very well equipped. We are already looking at booking our next camping trip back here in September.

Such a great location and very close to home. The kids absolutely love it as there’s so many activities for them to do in the park. The billabong sites beautiful.

Will be back

We’ve just come back from our camping trip there, and honestly, it wasn’t enough — we’re already wishing we could go back.

It was our first time visiting, and we were blown away. The waterpark is absolutely stunning, and learning about the history behind the place made the experience even more special. Our campsite was right in front of the billabong , and waking up to that view was incredible. The kids were completely mesmerised by all the aquatic life, we’d definitely coming back.
Amazing park, the kids loved the water park and pool. The cabin was very spacious and well appointed. The park is an easy trip to shops whether walking or driving.
Very friendly staff, convenient locations, and clean and tidy sites, with all the facilities. Will be a fixed location for family camping.

About as top tier as you get at the Tweed Heads Park. Everything was clean, green, peaceful and friendly. Ground and cleaning crew very friendly and easy going, office people fantastic to communicate with. Amber was amazing with her friendliness, energy, can do attitude, even when our plans changed slightly, she went above and beyond and never missed a beat in her smile and positiveness. You were their priority. Definitely recommend a stay at this park.
The most beautiful family holiday park. The cabins are so beautifully clean and new. The pools and slides are so much fun for young and old.

great place for kids can let them run around free, like free range chickens.
good sites not packed in like sardines, and cabins clean and well layed out. staff is exceptional, will be returning as have in past.
thanks again

Amazing friendly welcoming and very helpful staff. Easy parking with pleanty of room for all sites. Clean facilities, great amenities from the standard pool to the slide pool with toilet and shower area. Also great activities for all ages for the family here.
A great camp kitchen facility and well thought of seating indoors and out doors.
Great food venders on site with awesome prices, flavour and size meals, bang for back and family favourite.

The location isn't the best, let's get that out of the way first.

But the rest of the whole experience was spectacular.
Great sites
Wonderful little lake
The best clean and modern facilities
Cool they do food trucks (nachos were average though)
The friendliest staff
Kids and adults loved the water slides

There is a reason why all the reviews are so positive, even though its location isn't perfect.
We enjoyed everything about our stay at Billabong Big4. Will definitely stay again when up that way.  Excellent staff service, excellent site, excellent facilities.
It brought all of our families together it was the best 5 stars

We just had a fantastic stay at Big4 Tweed Billabong. We stayed 4 nights in the Gumnut Villa and it was amazing. Big enough for 2 families and perfect for the little ones with the private yard and gate to keep them closed in! The park itself was very clean and tidy, the kids were more than entertained with the pool, waterpark, hire equipment and kids activities the park puts on. They loved doing the colouring in competition everyday (which was also nice downtime for the mums)
The park is close to a cafe, parks, restaurants etc. The staff were so friendly and accomodating and the villa was in great condition. Only downfall is that 2/3 bedrooms don’t have aircon and it’s HOT!! In saying that, would definitely stay again, I’d just pack pedestal fans!! Highly recommend Big4 Tweed Billabong! :)

We had a billabong tent site and it was fantastic (apart from the mozzies) they love me.  Facilities were clean and tidy, staff were helpful and friendly
Great idea having the food trucks there.
Both rooms and facilities were immaculate. By far the best family park My family and I have ever been to. Perfect.

We stayed three nights at BIG4 Tweed Heads and loved every minute of it.

The cabin was spotless, comfortable and well equipped, with everything we needed for a relaxing family stay. It felt private and peaceful while still being close to all the action.

The facilities are outstanding. The pool area is fantastic and the waterslides were a huge highlight. Our toddler had the best time going between the slides, splash areas and the cubby house, which is such a thoughtful addition for little ones. There is plenty to keep kids entertained while still allowing parents to unwind.

The entire park is clean, well maintained and clearly designed with families in mind. The location is perfect, positioned between the Gold Coast and Kingscliff so you truly get the best of both worlds. Beaches, cafes and shops are all within easy reach, yet it still feels like a proper holiday escape.

It is the ideal mix of comfort, fun and convenience. We will absolutely be back.

Big 4 Tweed Heads is one of our favourite get away locations. Our very active teen loves the water park, pool and  jumping pillow, and thebillabong is great for the whole family to enjoy kayak & fishing. We get a cabin by the billabong and I love sitting on the screened deck having a break while watching the kids.  The cabins and park are always well presented, and the pop-up food trucks are convenient. The location is also great for day trips - if you feel the need to leave the park.

Stunning park, very safe with electric gates, grounds were amazing,  pop up food vans + coffee van.
Swam everyday. 100% recommended.

Ein sehr schöner Campingplatz mit zwei Pools.
Sehr schöne Küche mit Aufenthaltsraum und immer Musik in den Sanitärbereichen.
Fantastic family park

This is the best holiday park I’ve ever stayed at!

The facilities are awesome and there’s so much for the kids to do. The waterpark and pools are so much fun, even for the big kids. The billabong was a highlight for us, especially hiring a kayak and fishing.

We stayed in a Duranbah cabin and it was amazing. 3 large bedrooms, super spacious, really comfortable beds and had all the comforts of home.

The Food trucks were delicious, the daily kids activities were great, and the go-karts… all of it was a hit. My son particularly loved the Kids Club sessions. The live music on the camp kitchen deck was a beautiful way to end our stay.

The staff here are next level- so friendly and always going above and beyond. They truly made our stay.

We couldn’t fault anything. We’ll definitely be back!

A beautiful family friendly holiday spot. So many activities to keep the kids happy and busy. The slides at the water park and in the pool are a highlight. The kids club entertained the kids for an hour of craft, cooking and fun while mum and dad relaxed. The cabins are stunning, spacious and well maintained and the staff are friendly and helpful with any requests.

When we arrived the kids eyes lit up seeing there was a billabong they could swim and fish in got them all excited. Once they discovered the water park they didn’t want to leave. The biggest problem they had was waking up and deciding what to do. Did we go fishing or did we go swimming. Fantastic park. Friendly staff, so helpful. Clean facilities. Definitely will be back.
Thank you to all the staff.

The location and facilities available in the Holiday Park were fantastic. We had booked a family holiday weekend including a 5 year old birthday.
Exceptional service, the staff were very helpful and went out of their way to make our stay and birthday celebrations unforgettable.

The holiday park is in such a convenient location, making it perfect for families. It’s only a 10 minute drive to Aldi, Coles, and Woolworths, and just 10 minutes to Point Danger and the beautiful beaches.

It is close to the Gold Coast airport which means airplanes will be taking off and landing everyday, but we love planes so we enjoyed watching them!

The waterpark and pool were a huge highlight — the kids had an absolute blast! Mum was a bit scared of waterslides but then found one she enjoyed, then we couldn’t get her to leave! 🤣  Dad tried going down one of the waterslides and the water stopped in the middle, he had to drag himself down the rest of the way! 🤣
It took us a long to time to figure out that there is a button to activate the water!

We also loved the Friday movie nights by the pool, which created such a fun family atmosphere. We cooked in the cabin some delicious Mexican food which we ate during the movie - everyone was jealous because it smelt delicious-!!  Also, make sure to bring your popcorn as the Mini Mart in the reception doesn’t sell any.

Dessert and food trucks visit often, which was a lovely treat. The ice cream🍦 is delicious! The Sea Salt Caramel and Macadamia is definitely a recommendation!

There are so many activities available, including kayaking, go-karting, and stand-up paddle boarding for only $10 per hour. Kayaking in the lake was amazing, especially after we saw the stingray swimming underneath us! 😮
Don’t worry 😉 nothing dangerous.
If you go Go - karting, make sure you are high on carbohydrates because you will need a lot of leg power for the two person kart. After we finished, our legs immediately turned to jelly.
But it was definitely worth it!!

The kids enjoyed the jumping pillow, playground, and outdoor play zone with games like ping pong, mini golf, giant chess, and sport courts for soccer, basketball, or cricket. Equipment can be hired easily from reception, and there’s even a pickleball court.

The Sprinkles Bakehouse and Playground were fun since the kids had supervised cooking and craft sessions in the bakehouse.

The camp kitchen and BBQ area were great for family meals, and fishing in the lake was another enjoyable option.

Another fun thing was that we were visited by Australian Water Dragons each morning. Apparently they were playing hide and seek because they were hiding then chasing each other. At the end of the day, they were always waiting for us to give them something to eat. 😋

One small suggestion would be to add cushions to the outdoor, reclining chairs in the cabin, as they were very uncomfortable.
Dad loves reading outside with his coffee, but after 30 minutes of sitting in that chair, he almost broke his coccyx. 🤣

Overall, this place is fantastic for families, and we can’t wait to come back.
Highly recommended!!

The Faithful Travellers are ready for their next adventure and for their next review!

Great spot for families, water park is amazing.
Food trucks are awesome 🤙🏽🤙🏽🤙🏽

Everything about our stay was absolutely outstanding. The thoughtful touches, such as the daily activities for the children, made the experience even more enjoyable. The facilities were fantastic and so well spread throughout the park that even over a very busy Australia Day weekend, there was always somewhere to go with plenty of space. Combined with the incredibly friendly, bubbly, and welcoming staff throughout the park, it created a truly wonderful atmosphere.
Really great place with fantastic facilities. Kids loved every minute. Staff were extremely helpful and friendly. A credit to the organisation. Well done.

The staff was very friendly.

However, the campground was incredibly expensive. We paid $190 for one night. It was also very noisy due to the airplanes and the highway. In the last six weeks, we've stayed at countless campgrounds and paid only a third of that price each time.
I would have given 5 stars but we had no dinning table overall it was an excellent Park for young and old

We returned to Tweed Billabong after a long 14 years.. There has been so many upgrades since our last visit. Camp sites are still on the smaller side, but can absolutely be utilised to max if you are willing to put in the thoughts..
Amenities were great & clean, 11 yr old loved hearing stories of our last visit, and yes we were reminded of the planes & Highway 😆 but is it really that bad? To us it was just white noise..
our last holiday we only had a billabong to camp at & swim in so well done on the upgrades guys..
We took our own paddleboard and due to the waterpark & pools it was used for 2.5 seconds 😆
We will be back with the rest of the family next time.

One of the nicest caravan parks we’ve been to, with plenty of greenery and beautifully manicured sites. The views of the lake were amazing. The pools were beautiful and clean, and the large water park was a big hit.
The camp kitchen and camp lounge had an upmarket, restaurant-style vibe. We absolutely loved the treehouse — at night it was all lit up and looked stunning.
The food trucks in the afternoons were fantastic, with amazing food and very friendly owners. It was always a pleasure visiting them and a great bonus when we just wanted to relax instead of cooking.
On the downside, the toilets were not kept very clean during the last few days of our stay and appeared to be cleaned only once in the morning, which wasn’t enough for a fully booked caravan park.
Another negative was the café inside the park next to the camp kitchen (Billabong Café). It was only open from 7am to 12pm, and unfortunately one staff member lacked basic customer service skills. A friendly smile and kind interaction in the morning can really make a difference, and this was disappointing.
Overall we had a great holiday and we'd definitely come back

Our children loved this place, so much room for them to play.|Our accommodation was excellent, only thing would have been handy was a dryer in our apartment.|The staff were so friendly helpful and professional.|Was that good we stayed another night.|Thanks for the great time

We had a great time staying in a Duranbah Cabin.  There were 4 adults and 4 kids and the location in the park is great, close to the water park. The kids had such a good time after the 4th day they were looking to go to bed at around 6 in the evening. All the running around and swimming just wore them out in a good way. No boredom no searching for electronic devices to keep them entertained. We loved it and there wasnt enough hours in the day....  The only downside was the odd plane that flew over head, but being travelers it's a good sign for us.... We read people say the freeway was terrible but we thought it just a light hum in the distance and if we didnt stop and listen for it we hardly even noticed it during the day over the kids having a great time and at night its hardly noticeable.....  We loved the fact we could go to the water slides and just let the kids have a great time, these slides were big enough to even cater to teenagers having a good time, or we could let the toddlers loose and they had a great time.....

We (2 Adults + 2 Children (5 & 3) stayed over new years. The park was full and so much fun. Facilities are very clean and well maintained. Our cabin was really clean and well presented.

The kids absolutely loved the pool & water park. We adults enjoyed it too.

Yes, you are in the flight path but you just learn to deal with it. Highly recommend the park and would happily stay again.

What a great place, so much to do for the children and everything a parent needs for a successful holiday.
We made great use of the free function rooms to bring everyone (30 + family members) together for dinner. You literally don't have to leave the park for anything. The facilities and the whole park are so clean, the staff are friendly and helpful.
Highly, highly recommend ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

We stayed here for 8 nights in December 2025. Honestly the friendliest staff we have ever encountered at a caravan park. I cannot compliment the staff enough! They were more than happy to hear about my sons fishing adventures and helped us out with questions. We have our own van with amenities, but used the toilets occasionally and they were always clean and well serviced. Waterslides were fun. Pool was a lovely chilled environment. But the highlight is the dam/ lake, We spent time paddle boarding, kayaking and fishing. It was great to do this and not have to leave the caravan park. I found this park due to the dam had a better vibe for families with a variety of age ranges. The musicians that played were excellent and created a really nice atmosphere. Food trucks were great as well! Its also an easy spot to try other things in the area. We ventured up to Sea World, had lunch at Kirra hotel (amazing food!) with walks along the coastline and did a day out on a pontoon boat which was great fun. We will be back (potentially next Christmas!!). We found alot of the kids and families were friendly and our son in particular was always out playing with the other kids - he had an absolute ball (aged 10). Park had a super relaxed vibe which you don't always get, and you can tell its because the staff put in a super effort to keep that feel and everyone happy.

Overall it’s the best hoilday park
Staff are friendly and amber is a great young lady:)

Absolute hidden gem. Fantastic amenities that are well built and maintained. On-site kitchen over the billabong is amazing. The billabong itself and the pontoon with kayak and SUP hire is a great idea. The pool is fantastic, same as the two playgrounds with great features. The jumping pad is always a win. The parks is easily laid out, most sites are easily caravan reversible, though some can be tight. The cabins are air conditioned and lovely. There's plenty of snacks in the camp shop, but theres also a cafe on site which is nice. Theres a nearby pub and Dan Murphy's as well. Great location, helpful staff and great cabin rooms. I'll be bringing the caravan back this year too.
Great park, so much to do. We stayed in a cabin which was lovely, well equipped and even had a coffee machine. Kids spent hours in the pool and on the water slides.
Location is noisey with the highway and overhead planes but very popular with the families. With two pools, separate water park and a Lagoon the water is a great draw card for families.

Absolutely stunning place. Got a spot instantly via phone call during Christmas. Stayed next to the lake and the view was perfect the atmosphere was amazing. Not too rowdy being that time of the year. Has a pool and waterpark for the kids. Close to all the shops of need. Absolutely perfect place.
Very nice park well organized staff very friendly loved it 👌

Absolutely 💯 loved it here.
Place is so clean, welcoming and beautiful atmosphere.
I will return again without a doubt.
Excellent place to stay. Family orientated, friendly people and staff. Facilities are clean and well maintained. 👍👍

This is our second visit, and I’m honoured to have recommended such an amazing place to my friends.

The facilities are clean, well maintained, and offer a great variety of options that keep the kids happily entertained while parents can relax and enjoy their time too.

The front desk staff are incredibly friendly and helpful — it always feels like they know exactly what you need before you even ask.

Thank you to all the staff at BIG4 Tweed Heads. We can’t wait to visit again.
Fantastic place to go with family and kids, plenty to do, love the lake for paddle boarding. Will be going back with our camper trailer.

We stayed for a long weekend and it was lovely.
Great communication with the staff.
Very friendly staff.
The Duranbah cabin was lovely & clean.
Plenty of things to do for the children & big kids.
What an awesome spot! The Billabong is beautiful, there are so many activities for the kids, we had a wonderful stay. Thank you!
Spent most of the year travelling and this year and this is one of the best parks we have stayed at.

Big 4 Tweed Billabong was a surprising delight. The staff were lovely! The park was immaculate. There was so much to do. The rooms were great, so clean and had everything we needed! This is one of my favourite big 4's by far. Thanks TB team for a great although short stay you were all lovely. We will be back for a longer stay.

My only real feedback is - that because of the location, a dining option in the park would be amazing, the brunch cart was closed because we were there mid week outside of holidays, besides evertthing was beyond fantastic!!

We stayed in the Kirra cabin and, for us, the main bed was uncomfortable but that's personal preference .
Large sites, great facilities for other adults and kids. Loved the waterslide area. Great live music in the afternoon.
A wholly positive experience but most notably because of the super-friendly and helpful staff. Really impressive and most welcome for someone just starting a tour in Australia.

We had an amazing holiday and will definitely be coming back!
The location is great, it’s quiet enough and Tweed Heads is perfect for day trips and exploring. The rooms were budget friendly, nothing crazy fancy but great for a sturdy family holiday. All staff was incredibly polite and helpful. Looks, slides and amenities were unreal- they all made for the best family holiday. Our children are 1-9 years old and there was something for everyone. Take the trip, 10/10 recommend!!
Great family friendly park, lots of activities for kids

Spent an extra long weekend at Tweed Billabong and it didn’t disappoint!
We arrived a little early and the front reception staff were more than willing to help accommodate us, our cabin was ready and we are able to check earlier. Staff were friendly and always really helpful.

Our daughter absolutely loved the facilities, tried everything the park had to offer within the first day and didn’t stop the whole trip.

The cabin (Cabarita) was ideally located in the park with a short stroll to all activities. Was clean and perfect for the 3 of us with plenty of space.

Can not recommend Tweed Billabong highly enough. Will definitely be returning.
Average facilities, overpriced in most of the year especially holiday periods. Small sites. Would not recommend.
We had ensuite site, facilities clean. The pool and water park is great for the kids.

We loved our stay here. The service here by all staff was incredible too. So friendly & professional. And even the attention to detail was amazing. Like getting texts each day for what activities were on or when the food trucks were arriving and what they were serving. Unfortunately, it rained majority of the time we were there. But the staff went around hanging out pictures to paint, with paint, paint brushes and lollies to all the kids. I thought that was such a special touch. Everything in the park was super cleaned and well maintained. The only thing I would suggest is potentially having fishing rods for hire as well as all of the other kayaks & go karts etc. or having a ‘fishing’ activity. My kids would have loved that. But I don’t own any equipment haha. Rooms were so spacious and clean. And beds, pillows & doonas were so comfy. We had great sleeps every night :)
And only other suggestion would be maybe having some sort of rain proof areas for kids to play in/under if it does rain :)

This is our second year staying at Big4 Tweed Billabong and we will be back again. We stayed in the Duranbah cabin this time and it was beautiful, clean and well maintained. Close to the water park and billabong with a short walk to the pool. |There is so much for the children to do and they love making new friends, decorating cookies in the classes, poolside movies and the food trucks are such a great idea to have on offer some nights. We got a great deal stay 4 pay 3 which was first value and I hope they do that deal next year. We have been trying different big 4s and this is a favourite amongst the children and myself. Our grandparents also stayed on the caravan site overlooking the Billabong and loved it. |Can’t recommend enough for families.

My extended family of 15 ( adults and children) stayed for the weekend in 2 x 3 bedroom cabins. We were really impressed by the quality of the modern and well appointed cabins, even the linen and pillows were excellent.|The park facilities were exceptional and were perfect for our 6 children ranging in ages from 1 to 7. We also enjoyed the food trucks and hair braiding which were surprising add ons. We had a thoroughly memorable weekend and would enthusiastically recommend this to anyone.
very comfortable and clean park

We had to cancel our booking due to last weekend’s severe weather warnings and local flooding. Unfortunately, the park wasn’t flexible with their cancellation policy. As a young family, we were disappointed that there wasn’t a bit more understanding given the circumstances. Hopefully, future guests are shown a little more care.

We love Big 4 Tweed Billabong, they have the best amazing staff Lisa, Amber, Tayla and many more - who always welcome us back with warmth and love, remembering our children names and making our stay so fun, memorable! They are the best in their daily activities, the staff mentioned always go to the extra mile and care for each of us. A big thank you to Chris and team, see you back again! We return every year just to see you all ! Lots of Love from Jay, Kye, Storm and family

We had our sons birthday here in Feb 2025. Loved it that much we rebooked there and then for October. Had another great stay and have rebooked for next year Oct. Facilities are excellent and the Billabong was our kids favourite to swim out to the pontoon. What would be even greater is a zip line into the Billabong. Ablutions are great and modern with superb rain shower heads. The only negative is the traffic noise and aeroplane noise but the positives outweigh the negatives. We'll be back on a more regular basis and the ladies at reception were super friendly and welcoming upon our arrival. Keep up the great work. Note; We stayed at site 150 and the ground is super hard, you'll need reinforced tent pegs as the plain silver slender ones just bend when trying to get them in the ground.

Super friendly reception staff. Fantastic assortment of activities available. Clean facilities. Amazing communal kitchen/dining and lounge room facility overlooking the billabong. Very family friendly.

Park is amazing, the customer service really enhances all aspects of staying here. Amazing facilities and so much for kids to do. Special Mention to Natalie on reception, she is amazing and was a pleasure to deal with. Thanks Big 4 Tweed.

We had a great time with family and friends. Very clean, great waterpark and an overall nice environment. Only downside is the planes over head and M1 close which creates some noise but other than that I’d definitely stay again!
Awesome place and facilities, lots to do, great location.

This BIG4 Holiday Park has everything you could ever want for the kids and adults,
It’s got a massive lake that you can swim and use paddle boards in. It’s got a little relaxing lagoon for the adults. It’s even got a coffee shop. And The camp kitchen is beautiful and has a Veranda overlooking the lake..
I’d highly recommend this park to anybody just travelling through or even if you’re looking for a holiday,
I would happily stay here for two weeks .
Unban me from the holiday park I did nothing :(
Our 3rd ‘Family get together’ at the park. Fantastic staff and great facilities for all ages. Back again next year 👍

We had such a fun stay with our two kids! Our Billabong Spot 153 had the dreamiest north-facing views — pure nature vibes… until the M1 and airplanes reminded us we were basically glamping on an airport runway ✈️😂

The facilities are fantastic — resort-style pools with a slide, water park, go-karts, jumping pillow, sup, kayak and great playgrounds. The daily kids’ activities, pizza truck and Saturday movie night were a huge hit too. Everything was clean, organised and well-kept.

Jen and Donny at reception were lovely and flexible, though Natalie could work on her customer charm. Paying $30 for late checkout felt a bit steep, especially after a stormy first day and delayed movie night that kept the little ones up past bedtime. Also we didn’t get to try the Sprinkle Bakehouse, as they couldn’t cater for vegans. Hopefully next time they’ll have a few plant-based goodies on offer!

All in all, a great family spot packed with fun and thoughtful touches — just needs a few tweaks: more shade over the jumping pillow, extra sun loungers and umbrellas by the pool, a fire pit to roast marshmallow and a little more flexibility with checkout.

As for the sound pollution? Let’s just say if you close your eyes, you can almost pretend the roaring highway and plane engines are a soothing ocean breeze. Almost 😉
Wonderful staff, great range of facilities for children. Our cabin was beautiful and clean. Highly recommend for families!

Nice caravan park.
Security was good , except we got locked out at 8pm after coming home. Rang after hours number and no one answered. Husband had to jump fence to get code from unit.
Pool was lovely. Water slide park was fantastic for everyone. Camp kitchen was best one we have ever seen.
Might just to get some new kayaks  as they leak.
Showers were great.....
It's a great spot on the tweed River however,  there are loads of midgies! If you react to midgies then I'd say avoid the River side camp spots.

Amazing facilities for the kids, friendly staff. You get used to all the planes landing at the nearby airport.

We had a van site which had plenty of room, great park for kids. A bit of aeroplane noise but hardly noticeable with all stuff to do

The cabin was great..but shower walls and curtain needs a good clean.
Lady at reception was amazing and so helpful in every way.
Such a lovely camp ground. Just a pitty about the planes and the noisey ppl at night.

We took our 3 young grandchildren to this caravan park.  All staff were absolutely amazing and nothing was too much trouble.  Our main contact was with Amber, however, all staff were great.  There were lots of activities for the kids and the ensuited site was clean and in a good position.  Our grandkids loved there time at the park
Fantastic family holiday...  It's the 2nd year for our family staying with you... The kids enjoy the daily colouring comp, the pool, slides n canoeing in the billabong..

We had a fantastic multi- generational long weekend stay. The reception staff were absolutely wonderfully friendly and helpful (shout out to Jen and Amber in particular), the facilities clean and tidy, the coffee team were cheerful and the sites (we had 2 with Astro -turf) were great for old and young feet alike! We enjoyed a cuppa on the deck and pool time was fun. We had a powered tent site  and an ensuite site close to each other which suited the family well.
The only downside (which the park can do nothing about) is the airplane and motorway noise but you do get used to it in the main).
Overall a great brief getaway for our family. Thanks.
Robyn & Matt.

Friendly and super helpful staff,  fun kids’ activities, very well-kept amenities, a food truck dinner option every night of our stay and a short drive to shops and beaches. We took our caravan and it was easy enough access with all the power and water right where they’re needed. Loved our stay and will be back asap!

From the moment we arrived, the staff were so welcoming and accommodating to our family. The cabin was spotless, spacious, and well equipped, making our stay super comfortable. The facilities were fantastic, the kids loved the playground, pool, and daily activities that kept them entertained. Everything around the park was clean, easy to access, and family friendly. We also loved the peaceful location, which was still close to shops, beaches, and attractions. We had such a great experience and can’t wait to come back.

From the moment we arrived, the staff were so welcoming and accommodating to our family. The cabin was spotless, spacious, and well equipped, making our stay super comfortable. The facilities for families and children are wonderful with the pools and water parks, pickleball, mini golf, soccer etc. The check in and out and vehicle entry and exit are all seamless as it is all automated. We had a billabong site. Our camper site was difficult to setup due to an overhead tree and getting the boat rack in a position so we could setup with the annex not on the road made it longer to get into position than to actually setup.

Park facilities are great. Amenities great. Billabong was great fun with the SUPs. People fishing all over was frustrating. Signs say fish from Western side only but that's clearly not enforced. Lines being casted next to people kayaking, on SUPs and swimming.

Just be aware also. Noisey !!! The M1 is buzzing right beside the park. The real kicker! There are planes taking off at the nearby airport right over the park up until 11pm every night. Can feel it not just hear it.

Absolutely loved our stay at the Big 4 in tweed!
Staff were wonderful, food van and coffee van were amazing.
Kids had an absolute ball on the water slides.
Will definitely be returning!

Noise:
1.Very close to M1 Freeway so very hard to sleep at night and trucks wake you up in early morning. You can hear the sound 24/7

2. Right underneath Aircraft path so more noise makes it hard to sleep and carry on a conversation when passing overhead,

Cost:
Very expensive when compared with two other parks, Tweed Holiday Park and River Retreat Home & Holiday Park within 1klm east and west of this park which have direct open river access, boat ramp, closer to town & bus stop and cafe

Flexibility:
Even though it carries the branding of Big4 they have there own set of rules and once they have your money, they will not credit or refund,

Telephone coverage & free Wi-Fi:
Telstra has limited coverage
Optus has no coverage
Free-WiFi drops in and out

I was told to go to camp kitchen and coverage maybe better which was very inconvenient every time I wanted to make or receive a telephone call or browse on web

Great park for families - kids are having a great time. Sadly the location next to highway is really terrible. It might be better in a sound proof caravan but a pop up or tent, the highways doesn’t stop. To add to noise, the spot is directly under the plane flight path. I have hardly slept due to noise. Even my kids said it was so noisy and kept getting woken up. Such a Shane because staff is great and facilities great but I won’t be back due to noise.
What a place! The kids and I had so much fun staying at BIG4 Tweed Billabong. The staff were amazing and the facilities were fantastic. Definitely coming back!

The amenities at the park itself are great, especially the waterpark, soccer field and tree house. The location is where it lost points for us. The highway noise and aircraft noise spoiled our experience. If you’re not sensitive to noise, it’s a great place.

We were quite disappointed to find out that it’s $150 a night for a powered campsite in the school holidays? I don’t understand how that’s justified. There is demand, and then there is just blatantly ripping families off. We have spent the last 2 years travelling Australia and stayed at many Big4s, it’s so sad that this is our only bad review on any of the ones we’ve ever stayed at.
Anyone who books in the future, be aware that this park is right next to the freeway & is on a main flight path so there is noise is every direction. $150 a night is not okay.
I wouldn't have stayed here if I knew about the noise from the highway and jets from the airport. But it's fine other than that.

Had a wonderful stay here with our family of 4 in a cabin. There are so many activities to keep the family entertained you wouldn't even need to leave and plenty of space for outdoor activities. Kids loved the go karts which you can hire for $10 an hour. The amenities are also clean and well maintained. We will be back!

I just wanted to pass on my sincere thanks for a wonderful stay at your park. Beautiful park will return for a holiday

The staff were absolutely lovely — the ladies at the front desk were so kind and amazing with our kids, always happy to help. They were also super understanding when we asked to switch rooms for a bit more space, which we really appreciated.

More than met our expectations. Staff very friendly and helpful. Our cabin was beautifully decorated , clean and comfortable. The layout was easy to navigate with great facilities for adults and kids. Added bonus are the food trucks . The water theme park is fantastic.

Currently staying here for work and have to say the park is neat and tidy. Well looked after, good amenities and nice areas to relax.

Not good, no consideration for dogs.This park really behind the times. Wake up.

BIG4 Tweed Billabong Holiday Park:

Amazing Stay at BIG4 Tweed Billabong!
We had an incredible time at BIG4 Tweed Billabong Holiday Park. The staff were extremely helpful, friendly, and went above and beyond to make our stay perfect. We really enjoyed staying here,  we have a Mercedes van and a small trailer,  plenty of room. The park is lovely as is the staff.

We unexpectedly discovered BIG4 Tweed Heads when looking for a quick overnight stop. Wish we had a whole week there!  So much for the kids to do and yet it feels secluded with all the trees and the billabong.  The outdoor kitchen is the best we've seen. 5 stars!

Perfect ambience to stay and rest of the day with kid. good location and pleasant surroundings amenities.

We had an excellent 5 day stay over the school holidays. Clean and spacious cabins and lots for the kids to do. The staff were excellent and specifically Amber at the front desk was warm and helpful. Give the girls at the front desk a raise please Big 4! Thank you for a great stay.
Fantastic park, great water park for the kids and a heated pool. Ensuited suites are great.
Nice park unfortunately not pet friendly

I keep saying as we travel around Australia, that a reception generally sets the tone. Awesome, clean and friendly.

The park is clean and well presented with an awesome camp kitchen and barbecue facilities all set around a lagoon fed by the local river.  While the park is very strongly focused on families, please as an older couple you will not be disappointed with some quiet areas.

End of the day nice to see the bathrooms still clean. They are modern and the showers are hot with nice overhead rain style outlets.

They have a food theme on weekends that are presented by local mobile businesses.

Nice park and credit to management and staff.

Don’t let the aircraft turn you off from Gold Coast airport and we found our bay a bit tight to get into with our 8.5 metre length.

But have to give it a 5.  Another awesome park on our trip.

Such a wonderful park so much for the kids to do for all ages. We have three kids and are travelling full time and they all had so much fun even in the colder weather. The amenities were amazing with a beautiful big fireplace for those winter nights. The water park was a big hit, along with the heated pool, fishing in the billabong, constant activities provided by the park, jumping pillow, playground plus more. I just could not fault this caravan park, one of the best places we have stayed to date. Highly recommend.
We had a wonderful stay our cabin had everything we needed was very clean the park facilities were fantastic and the grounds were so clean kids loved the water park and stand up paddle boarding
Great staff and facilities just to many jets and highway traffic noise
What an absolute awesome place to stay!
Fantastic! 5 star all round
Was an epic experience for me and my family.
Stayed on caravan site. Planes at 6:00 in the morning but otherwise a great park.
We camped in site 30, across from the billabong. Easy to get our van in and fantastic park for kids. Water park, heated pool, jumping pillow, parks and school holiday activities. Highly recommend.

Awesome place! Facilities are great. Lots to do within the park, if you had a young family you wouldn't need to leave!. Lovely lagoon that you can fish in, stand up paddle boards and kayaks for hire. Little water park and food trucks on the week end. Planned children's activities as well. Fantastic little place.
We are a large family with our 11 grandchildren.  Everything you need is close by.  The water features in the play ground kept the kids entertained.

This is a great holiday park for families, amenities were clean and well maintained.
This park had everything great camp kitchen with two lounge rooms with large televisions and veranda with seating to look out over the  lake where the kids swam, fished and canoeing. There was a large waterpark. They had an onsite coffee shop for morning cappuccinos 😀 They organised children’s activities and had food vans come to site. Highly recommend

This is a favourite big4, my 3 kids all love this place, endless things for them to do and have fun. Water slide park, 2 pools, jumping pillow. Food trucks kids club. The only downfall is the plains. Airport is close so plains are very loud. In saying that the noise stops at 10pm.

Overall, this is a fantastic place to stay, with new, clean and well maintained amenities.

There's awesome entertainment for the kids in the form of a pool area and a water park in separate areas, as well as a park and the Billabong for water activities like stand up paddle boarding, canoeing and more.

Parking for visitors is limited, and the entrance is on a quiet residential street that is often frustrating to get down due to parked cars on the road making it single lane only - both directions at most times.

The wifi is fast, and blankets the park which is great and some cabins have individual wifi with smart TVs so you can use the streaming services, or Chromecast content.

The only downside was the park maintenance person who decided to pressure wash the back of our cabin on a Saturday afternoon after id just started a nap - did it have to be done right then?

Other than that, food trucks on a Friday/Saturday are great and movies in the camp kitchen is awesome.

Need to be aware of time zones NSW/GC. Other than that we loved it. Very child friendly and theres lots to do for them. Spent most time in the pools and water park. Staff were great and accommodating. We have already recommended to family and friends. 👌

Great park with plenty of activities to entertain the kids. With the little Cafe and food trucks on Friday and Saturday night, makes it a really easy and relaxing family holiday. Great location and felt very safe being gated.

This is an amazing caravan park. (Family orientated) One of our favourites. Heaps of facilities. Pool. Waterpark .jumping pillow. Kayaks. Paddle boards. Billabong. Pedal carts. Play ground
I actually didn't want to review because I don't want it to become more popular and harder to book...but fair is fair. Amazing for families.

What an amazing park for families. The facilities are updated, clean and well kept.

The most friendly reception staff of all the parks that we've visited!!!

Sadly Not what WE expected after all this Reviews. Just Something for Family. ON the Weekends it is very full, Lots of Kids. Kids playing Loud until late. Toilets Clean, after they get cleaned but with a Lot of People they get quickly dirty. Very Bad WiFi and No Service. Only good WiFi available at the camp kitchen.
Locals staying for a weekend for a 10th birthday. Everyone enjoyed, even the ones who don’t normal caravan park. Will be back for more staycations
Loved this place how much fun was it paddling on the billabong , the staff were super friendly everything so clean had an awesome time ❤️❤️ totally recommend

Great place for aviation fanatics !!,  awesome camp kitchen and showers

Such a good place for families! My kids loved it

The single spilt style air-conditioning unit in the living room meant that we couldn't close the bedroom doors to cool the entire cabin, which we would have preferred to run the fans. Only, the fan in the bunk bed room was far too noisy, and the fan in the queen size room sounded as if it was rolling away. We also came across quiet a few dirty kitchen items, not that Billabong are really at fault for this, rather the previous  people to have stayed and not cleaned properly prior to putting stuff away. Not the greatest place for mobile phone reception, and with the windows shut for the air-conditioning to work, it blocks any chance the WiFi has of working. This was a great experience tons of activities in the park and in close surrounding area even when raining it was still heaps of fun with the kids they loved fishing in the Billabong and going to the waterpark. it was an amazing experience I will be returning

Duranbah cabins are excellent but pricey but the only option for a family. Road and aircraft noise will bother you after 2 days. Permanent residents seem bothered by guests. Only one AC unit is very impractical and under powered. Lack of eves means windows closed with rain so it gets steamy in the bedrooms. Parking for 1 car and a small boat is standard. Unit 175 has room for 2 cars and a boat so is the pick.  Mozzies will eat you alive so verandah is a bit useless
Didn't stay in accommodation, had our camper, grounds well looked after. Customer service outstanding.

We stayed in the “fingal” cabin with 2 adults and 4 kids. The cabin was well thought out and equipt with everything we needed to prepared basic meals. We loved the lounges on the deck for chill time. The kids road their scooters on the roads inside the parks and it didn’t feel dangerous at all. We didn’t witness anyone driving fast in our time there.
The pools and water parks were awesome. There is defs enough to do!
I would recommend Tweed big 4 to friends and family. Even before arriving, we had someone call and ask if there was anything we needed to know before arriving.
Great place to entertain the kids. Loved it.

Wow what a beautiful place.  The girls at the front desk were so incredible.  I don’t think I’ve experienced such amazing customer service like this before, they truly went above and beyond.  The facilities were amazing and the accommodation was so beautiful.  Hope to go back some day.

We loved it here! The amenities were fantastic, lots to do and the kids loved it. The staff were super friendly and helpful, and provided excellent customer service. Our campsite was large, and close to everything. Highly recommend!

Awsome place great pools,  camp kitchen is amazing    friendly staff              plane's take a bit of geting used  to they do stop flying over at about 10pm  that's good and start back up at 7am     but me and my family will be back next year
Absolutely fabulous, perfect for All love it so much 6 x 🌟

Great park and good facilities ,pool    great for the kiddos
